Title: Scaffold monorepo with pnpm workspaces
Description:
User story:
As a developer, I want a clean monorepo so backend and frontend can be developed together.

Acceptance criteria:
- Root repo uses pnpm workspaces.
- Backend and frontend run independently.
- Root script can run both apps.
- Repo follows the agreed structure.

Tasks:
- Create radreport-ai root.
- Add pnpm-workspace.yaml.
- Add root package.json.
- Add .npmrc with shamefully-hoist=true.
- Add .gitignore.
- Create backend workspace.
- Create frontend workspace.
- Add dev scripts.

Title: Configure TypeScript, linting, and formatting
Description:
User story:
As a developer, I want consistent TypeScript and code quality rules across the project.

Acceptance criteria:
- Backend and frontend compile with strict TypeScript.
- Linting works in both workspaces.
- Formatting is consistent.
- Basic quality scripts exist.

Tasks:
- Configure backend tsconfig.
- Configure frontend tsconfig.
- Add ESLint.
- Add Prettier.
- Add lint, format, and typecheck scripts.
- Document conventions.

Title: Add environment configuration and startup validation
Description:
User story:
As a developer, I want safe environment configuration so secrets are never hardcoded.

Acceptance criteria:
- Backend fails fast when required env vars are missing.
- Frontend reads API URL from Vite env.
- Example env files exist.
- No secrets are committed.

Tasks:
- Create backend .env.example.
- Create frontend .env.example.
- Add backend env validation.
- Add frontend VITE_API_URL.
- Add README setup instructions.
- Ensure .env is ignored.

Title: Replace custom JWT auth with Supabase Auth
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want secure authentication handled by Supabase Auth.

Acceptance criteria:
- Users can register using Supabase Auth.
- Users can log in using Supabase Auth.
- Frontend stores Supabase session safely.
- Backend verifies Supabase JWTs.
- Custom users.password_hash flow is removed.

Tasks:
- Remove custom bcrypt/JWT auth plan.
- Configure Supabase Auth.
- Add frontend auth client.
- Create register flow.
- Create login flow.
- Create logout flow.
- Create session restore flow.
- Add backend middleware to verify Supabase JWT.
- Create profiles table linked to auth.users.

Title: Create profiles table and role model
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want my app profile connected to my Supabase Auth account.

Acceptance criteria:
- profiles.id references auth.users.id.
- Users can read/update their own profile.
- Role supports admin and user.
- RLS protects profile records.

Tasks:
- Create profiles migration.
- Add email, full_name, role, timestamps.
- Add RLS policies.
- Add profile creation trigger after signup.
- Add profile update flow.

Title: Implement protected frontend routes
Description:
User story:
As an authenticated user, I want protected app routes so private medical data is not exposed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unauthenticated users are redirected to login.
- Authenticated users can access app pages.
- Session loading state is handled.
- Logout clears local session.

Tasks:
- Create auth provider.
- Create protected route wrapper.
- Create login page.
- Create register page.
- Create logout action.
- Add session loading skeleton.
- Redirect users after login.

Title: Create core medical data migrations
Description:
User story:
As a developer, I want reliable database schemas for patients, reports, and treatments.

Acceptance criteria:
- Core tables exist.
- Tables use created_by linked to auth.users.id.
- Common query indexes exist.
- Duplicate report filenames per patient are blocked.

Tasks:
- Create patients table.
- Create radiology_reports table.
- Create treatment_records table.
- Add created_by UUID references auth.users(id).
- Add updated_at trigger.
- Add indexes.
- Add unique constraint on patient_id and filename.

Title: Enable RLS on all app tables
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want only my own patient data accessible.

Acceptance criteria:
- RLS is enabled on all app tables.
- Users can only access rows they own.
- Unauthorized cross-user access fails.
- Policies are manually tested.

Tasks:
- Enable RLS on profiles.
- Enable RLS on patients.
- Enable RLS on radiology_reports.
- Enable RLS on treatment_records.
- Add select/insert/update/delete policies.
- Write SQL tests for ownership behavior.

Title: Configure private Supabase Storage bucket
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want uploaded PDFs stored privately.

Acceptance criteria:
- reports bucket is private.
- Files are scoped by user and patient.
- Users cannot access another user’s files.
- No permanent public PDF URLs are stored.

Tasks:
- Create private reports bucket.
- Define storage path convention.
- Add Supabase storage policies.
- Store storage_path, not public URL.
- Remove public URL assumptions from schema.

Title: Generate signed PDF URLs on demand
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to view PDFs securely using temporary signed URLs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Signed URLs are generated only for authorized users.
- URLs expire.
- Frontend can open the PDF.
- Expired URLs can be regenerated.

Tasks:
- Create endpoint POST /api/reports/:id/signed-url.
- Verify report ownership.
- Generate Supabase signed URL.
- Return short-lived URL.
- Add View PDF action in frontend.
- Handle expired/failed URL states.

Title: Build patient CRUD API
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to create, view, update, and delete patient records.

Acceptance criteria:
- All patient routes are protected.
- Users only see their own patients.
- Inputs are validated.
- Search/filter/sort works.

Tasks:
- Create GET /api/patients.
- Create POST /api/patients.
- Create GET /api/patients/:id.
- Create PATCH /api/patients/:id.
- Create DELETE /api/patients/:id.
- Add search, stage, and sort filters.
- Add validation and error handling.

Title: Build patient list UI
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to browse and search my patients.

Acceptance criteria:
- Patient list loads from API.
- Search works.
- Stage filtering works.
- Loading, empty, and error states exist.

Tasks:
- Create PatientList page.
- Add search input.
- Add stage filter.
- Add sort dropdown.
- Add patient table/cards.
- Add loading skeleton.
- Add empty state.
- Add error state.

Title: Build add/edit patient dialog
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to add and edit patient information in a form.

Acceptance criteria:
- Required fields are validated.
- Patient can be created.
- Patient can be edited.
- UI updates after save.

Tasks:
- Create AddPatientDialog.
- Add patient form fields.
- Add frontend validation.
- Connect create mutation.
- Connect update mutation.
- Invalidate patient queries after save.

Title: Build patient detail page
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want a full patient profile with reports and treatments.

Acceptance criteria:
- Patient detail loads by ID.
- Header shows key medical data.
- Biomarker badges render.
- Tabs are available.

Tasks:
- Create PatientDetail.
- Add patient header.
- Add cancer stage badge.
- Add ER/PR/HER2 badges.
- Add Overview, Timeline, Treatments, and Comparison tabs.
- Load reports and treatments.

Title: Build secure PDF upload endpoint
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to upload a PDF report for a patient.

Acceptance criteria:
- Only PDFs are accepted.
- Max file size is 20 MB.
- Duplicate filename per patient is blocked.
- File is stored privately.
- Report record is created.

Tasks:
- Configure multer.
- Validate MIME type.
- Validate file extension.
- Validate max size.
- Verify patient ownership.
- Check duplicate report filename.
- Upload to private Supabase Storage.
- Create report record with pending status.
- Store storage path.

Title: Implement immediate report processing
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want my uploaded report processed immediately after upload.

Acceptance criteria:
- Upload triggers processing in the same flow.
- Status changes from pending to processing.
- PDF text is extracted.
- Claude analysis runs.
- Report is updated.
- Status becomes completed or failed.

Tasks:
- Create report processing service.
- Set status to processing.
- Extract PDF text.
- Call Claude analysis.
- Validate AI output.
- Store structured extraction.
- Store raw text.
- Store processing time.
- Set status to completed.
- On error, set status to failed.
- Return final report state.

Title: Build PDF parsing service
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want the app to extract text from uploaded PDF reports.

Acceptance criteria:
- PDF text is extracted reliably.
- Empty/unreadable PDFs fail gracefully.
- Raw text is stored privately.
- Errors are user-friendly.

Tasks:
- Add pdf-parse.
- Create pdfService.
- Extract raw text from uploaded file.
- Detect empty text.
- Handle encrypted PDFs.
- Handle malformed PDFs.
- Add parse failure errors.

Title: Build Claude analysis service
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want Claude to extract structured radiology report data.

Acceptance criteria:
- Claude uses claude-sonnet-4-20250514.
- Response is valid JSON.
- Extracted fields are stored.
- Invalid AI responses are handled safely.

Tasks:
- Add Anthropic SDK.
- Create claudeService.
- Implement report analysis prompt.
- Set max_tokens.
- Parse JSON response.
- Validate schema.
- Store BI-RADS, confidence, evidence, breast density, exam info, comparison, findings, recommendations, and red flags.

Title: Add evidence quote verification
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want AI evidence quotes verified against the original PDF text.

Acceptance criteria:
- Evidence quotes are checked against raw_text.
- Missing quotes are flagged.
- UI can show verification status.
- Unverified evidence does not appear as fully trusted.

Tasks:
- Build evidence verification utility.
- Normalize whitespace in report text.
- Normalize whitespace in evidence quotes.
- Check each quote against raw text.
- Store verification result.
- Add evidence_verified metadata.
- Add warning for unverified evidence.

Title: Generate patient-friendly report summary
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want a plain-language summary of report findings.

Acceptance criteria:
- Summary is 2-4 sentences.
- Summary is medically cautious.
- Summary includes disclaimer context.
- Summary is stored in report record.

Tasks:
- Create summary prompt.
- Add generateSummary service method.
- Generate summary after extraction.
- Store summary.
- Display in report detail.

Title: Add AI safety disclaimers and clinical review language
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want clear warnings that AI output does not replace clinical judgment.

Acceptance criteria:
- Disclaimer appears on report detail.
- Disclaimer appears on treatment comparison.
- Disclaimer appears on HowTo page.
- Language is clear and hard to miss.

Tasks:
- Write standard disclaimer copy.
- Add disclaimer component.
- Render in report detail.
- Render in treatment comparison.
- Render in HowTo page.

Title: Build reports list and cards
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to see uploaded reports and their processing status.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reports list loads from API.
- Status is visible.
- BI-RADS is visible when available.
- Red flag count is visible.
- Failed reports can be retried.

Tasks:
- Create ReportsList.
- Create ReportCard.
- Show filename.
- Show created date.
- Show status dot.
- Show BI-RADS badge.
- Show red flag count.
- Add retry action for failed reports.

Title: Build report detail slide-over
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to inspect extracted report details.

Acceptance criteria:
- Report detail opens in slide-over.
- BI-RADS is prominent.
- Evidence quotes are visible.
- Red flags are highlighted.
- PDF can be viewed securely.

Tasks:
- Create ReportDetail.
- Add red flag banner.
- Add BI-RADS hero card.
- Add exam info section.
- Add AI summary section.
- Add findings section.
- Add recommendations section.
- Add evidence quote display.
- Add evidence verification warning.
- Add View PDF action.
- Add Delete action.

Title: Build file dropzone
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to drag and drop a PDF onto the app.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dropzone accepts PDFs only.
- Dropzone is disabled without selected patient.
- Upload progress is shown.
- Processing status is shown after upload.

Tasks:
- Create FileDropzone.
- Add drag/drop handling.
- Add file validation.
- Add size validation.
- Add patient requirement.
- Show upload progress.
- Trigger immediate processing.
- Show success/failure state.

Title: Add report processing retry support
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to retry failed report processing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Failed reports show retry action.
- Retry reuses existing private PDF.
- Status updates correctly.
- Errors are preserved for debugging.

Tasks:
- Create POST /api/reports/:id/retry.
- Verify ownership.
- Fetch storage file.
- Re-run processing.
- Update status.
- Add retry button to UI.
- Show retry loading state.

Title: Store internal processing errors safely
Description:
User story:
As a developer, I want report failures debuggable without leaking PHI.

Acceptance criteria:
- Error code is stored.
- User sees safe error message.
- PHI is not logged.
- Raw Claude prompt is never logged.

Tasks:
- Add processing_error_code.
- Add processing_error_message.
- Sanitize error details.
- Add safe frontend error display.
- Add developer error ID.

Title: Build treatment CRUD API
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to manage treatment records for a patient.

Acceptance criteria:
- Treatment records can be created, read, updated, and deleted.
- Records are scoped to authenticated user.
- Records are linked to patients.

Tasks:
- Create GET /api/treatments.
- Create POST /api/treatments.
- Create GET /api/treatments/:id.
- Create PATCH /api/treatments/:id.
- Create DELETE /api/treatments/:id.
- Add validation.
- Verify patient ownership.

Title: Build treatment UI
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to view and manage treatments in the patient detail page.

Acceptance criteria:
- Treatments display in a patient tab.
- Users can add/edit/delete treatments.
- Treatment outcomes are visible.

Tasks:
- Create treatment tab.
- Create treatment form.
- Create treatment cards/list.
- Add edit action.
- Add delete action.
- Add loading/empty/error states.

Title: Build patient timeline
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want a timeline of diagnosis, reports, and treatments.

Acceptance criteria:
- Timeline combines patient, report, and treatment events.
- Events are sorted by date.
- Events are color-coded by type.
- Missing dates are handled gracefully.

Tasks:
- Create PatientTimeline.
- Add diagnosis event.
- Add report events.
- Add treatment events.
- Add follow-up events.
- Sort chronologically.
- Add event icons and colors.

Title: Build treatment comparison API
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want AI-assisted comparison of treatment options.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can compare up to 5 treatment options.
- Claude returns valid JSON.
- Output includes disclaimer.
- Output is clearly not a medical directive.

Tasks:
- Create POST /api/ai/compare-treatments.
- Validate max 5 options.
- Fetch patient context.
- Call Claude comparison prompt.
- Parse JSON response.
- Validate response schema.
- Return scores and recommendation.
- Include disclaimer.

Title: Build treatment comparison UI
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to enter treatment options and compare them visually.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can add up to 5 options.
- Comparison results render clearly.
- Disclaimer appears above AI output.
- Highest score is highlighted.

Tasks:
- Create TreatmentComparison.
- Add option inputs.
- Add compare button.
- Add loading state.
- Add error state.
- Render result cards.
- Render disclaimer above output.

Title: Build treatment comparison charts
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want visual charts for treatment comparison results.

Acceptance criteria:
- Scores render visually.
- Benefits/risks are readable.
- Duration and side effects are easy to compare.

Tasks:
- Create TreatmentComparisonCharts.
- Add score chart.
- Add scored cards.
- Add efficacy section.
- Add side effect section.
- Add duration comparison.

Title: Build consolidated report analysis API
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want multiple reports for a patient summarized together.

Acceptance criteria:
- API fetches completed reports for one patient.
- Claude summarizes progression and concerning patterns.
- Output includes recommendations and caution language.

Tasks:
- Create POST /api/ai/consolidate-reports.
- Verify patient ownership.
- Fetch completed reports.
- Fetch patient data.
- Call Claude consolidation prompt.
- Return consolidated analysis.

Title: Add deterministic trend detection
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want obvious changes across reports highlighted by code, not only AI.

Acceptance criteria:
- BI-RADS changes are calculated deterministically.
- New red flags are detected.
- Resolved findings are detected when possible.
- Risk increase is highlighted.

Tasks:
- Compare reports chronologically.
- Calculate BI-RADS trend.
- Detect highest BI-RADS.
- Detect new red flags.
- Detect recurring findings.
- Detect resolved findings where possible.
- Return trend metadata to frontend.

Title: Build consolidated report modal
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want a modal summarizing all reports for a patient.

Acceptance criteria:
- Modal shows aggregate report stats.
- Modal shows deterministic trends.
- Modal shows Claude summary.
- Modal allows JSON export.

Tasks:
- Create ConsolidatedView.
- Show total reports.
- Show latest BI-RADS.
- Show highest BI-RADS.
- Show red flag count.
- Show trend warnings.
- Show AI summary.
- Show report index.
- Add export JSON action.

Title: Build analytics dashboard
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want analytics across my patient population.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dashboard shows summary stats.
- Charts render correctly.
- Data is scoped to authenticated user.
- Empty states exist.

Tasks:
- Create PatientAnalytics.
- Add summary cards.
- Add Demographics, Diagnostic, and Treatment chart tabs.
- Add Recharts components.
- Add loading and empty states.

Title: Add analytics filters
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to filter analytics so charts are actually useful.

Acceptance criteria:
- Analytics can be filtered by date range.
- Analytics can be filtered by cancer stage.
- Analytics can be filtered by treatment type.
- Filters affect all charts.

Tasks:
- Add date range filter.
- Add cancer stage filter.
- Add treatment type filter.
- Wire filters to queries.
- Add clear filters action.

Title: Create audit logs table
Description:
User story:
As a system owner, I want sensitive actions logged for accountability.

Acceptance criteria:
- Audit table exists.
- User actions can be recorded.
- Audit logs do not expose PHI unnecessarily.
- Logs are protected by RLS/admin access.

Tasks:
- Create audit_logs table.
- Add id, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, metadata, created_at.
- Enable RLS.
- Add admin-only read policy.
- Add insert policy for backend service role.

Title: Log sensitive user actions
Description:
User story:
As a system owner, I want sensitive patient/report actions recorded.

Acceptance criteria:
- Uploads are logged.
- Report views are logged.
- Deletes are logged.
- Patient changes are logged.
- AI processing is logged.

Tasks:
- Create auditService.
- Log patient create/update/delete.
- Log report upload.
- Log report view.
- Log report delete.
- Log PDF signed URL generation.
- Log AI processing start/success/failure.
- Log treatment create/update/delete.

Title: Add PHI-safe application logging
Description:
User story:
As a developer, I want useful logs without exposing patient data.

Acceptance criteria:
- Raw PDF text is never logged.
- Claude prompts are never logged.
- JWT/session tokens are never logged.
- Sensitive fields are redacted.

Tasks:
- Add logger utility.
- Define redaction rules.
- Redact patient names.
- Redact raw report text.
- Redact tokens.
- Redact Claude request bodies.
- Add error IDs.

Title: Add de-identification mode before Claude calls
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want patient identifiers stripped before text is sent to Claude.

Acceptance criteria:
- Obvious identifiers are removed or replaced.
- Original raw text remains private in database.
- De-identified text is sent to Claude.
- Extraction still works after de-identification.

Tasks:
- Create de-identification service.
- Remove patient names.
- Remove DOB.
- Remove MRNs if present.
- Remove phone numbers.
- Remove addresses.
- Remove emails.
- Replace identifiers with placeholders.
- Store de-identification metadata.
- Send only de-identified text to Claude.
- Add tests for common PHI patterns.

Title: Export report extraction JSON
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to export structured report extraction data.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can export one report as JSON.
- Export only works for owned reports.
- Export includes extraction fields and verification metadata.

Tasks:
- Create export endpoint.
- Verify report ownership.
- Return JSON payload.
- Add frontend export button.
- Download JSON file.

Title: Export patient data bundle
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want to export a patient’s structured data.

Acceptance criteria:
- Export includes patient, reports, treatments, and consolidated analysis.
- Export excludes private PDF binary files by default.
- Export is scoped to owned patient only.

Tasks:
- Create patient export endpoint.
- Fetch patient.
- Fetch reports.
- Fetch treatments.
- Include AI summaries.
- Add frontend export action.
- Download JSON file.

Title: Export analytics CSV
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want analytics exported as CSV.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can export analytics data.
- CSV respects active filters.
- CSV contains no unauthorized data.

Tasks:
- Create analytics export endpoint.
- Add CSV generation utility.
- Apply filters.
- Add frontend export button.

Title: Build admin system health dashboard
Description:
User story:
As an admin, I want to monitor system usage and failures.

Acceptance criteria:
- Only admins can access dashboard.
- Dashboard shows processing stats.
- Dashboard shows recent failures.
- Dashboard shows usage metrics.

Tasks:
- Add admin route guard.
- Create admin dashboard page.
- Show total users.
- Show total reports.
- Show failed reports.
- Show average processing time.
- Show recent audit events.
- Show AI failure rate.

Title: Build admin user management
Description:
User story:
As an admin, I want to view users and roles.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin can view users.
- Admin can view profile roles.
- Admin can update user role if allowed.
- Non-admins cannot access user management.

Tasks:
- Create admin users endpoint.
- Add admin RLS/service checks.
- Create users table UI.
- Add role update action.
- Add audit logging for role changes.

Title: Add organization data model
Description:
User story:
As a clinic/team, we want multiple users to share patient records securely.

Acceptance criteria:
- Organizations exist.
- Users can belong to organizations.
- Patients can belong to organizations.
- Access is role-based.

Tasks:
- Create organizations table.
- Create organization_members table.
- Add owner/admin/clinician/viewer roles.
- Add organization_id to patients.
- Update RLS policies.
- Update queries to support organization scope.

Title: Add team sharing permissions
Description:
User story:
As an organization admin, I want to control who can access shared patient data.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admins can invite users.
- Members can access permitted records.
- Viewers cannot mutate data.
- Sharing events are audited.

Tasks:
- Add invite flow.
- Add member list.
- Add role management.
- Add permission checks.
- Add audit logs.
- Add frontend team settings page.

Title: Implement app layout and navigation
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want consistent navigation across the app.

Acceptance criteria:
- Layout uses sticky nav.
- User dropdown works.
- Navigation is responsive.
- Logout is available.

Tasks:
- Create Layout.
- Add sticky nav.
- Add logo.
- Add user dropdown.
- Add nav links.
- Add mobile nav.
- Add logout action.

Title: Implement light/dark mode
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want light and dark mode support.

Acceptance criteria:
- Theme toggle works.
- Theme persists.
- shadcn/ui components support both themes.
- Charts remain readable.

Tasks:
- Configure Tailwind dark mode.
- Configure shadcn theme.
- Add theme provider.
- Add theme toggle.
- Test core pages in both themes.

Title: Build Home dashboard
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want a home dashboard for quick upload and report review.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dashboard shows stats.
- Patient selector works.
- Upload is available.
- Recent reports are visible.

Tasks:
- Create Home.
- Add stat cards.
- Add patient selector.
- Add FileDropzone.
- Add ReportsList.
- Add recent activity.
- Add empty state.

Title: Build HowTo and medical disclaimer page
Description:
User story:
As a user, I want guidance on using the app safely.

- HowTo page explains app usage.
- Medical disclaimer is prominent.
- AI limitations are clear.

Tasks:
- Create HowTo.

- Add BI-RADS explanation.
- Add evidence quote explanation.
- Add AI limitation section.
- Add treatment comparison warning.
- Add privacy explanation.

Title: Build Axios API client
Description:

As a frontend developer, I want a reusable API client.

Acceptance criteria:
- API base URL is configured.
- Supabase access token is attached.
- 401s are handled.
- Errors are normalized.

Tasks:
- Create services/api.ts.
- Attach Supabase access token.
- Add response interceptor.
- Handle 401.

Title: Configure React Query
Description:

Description:

Title: Add backend API tests
Description:

As a developer, I want backend tests for critical flows.

Title: Add frontend tests
Description:

As a developer, I want frontend tests for key user flows.

Acceptance criteria:
- Auth pages are tested.
- Patient form is tested.

Title: Add Playwright E2E tests
Description:
User story:
